name: Chocolate Fudge Icing
recipeInstructions: This icing recipe is a candy recipe with milk added to make it into frosting. Bring to a boil in a large heavy pan, and cook covered for 2-3 minutes until the steam washes down any crystals that may have formed. Uncover, reduce heat, and cook without stirring to soft-ball stage (238°F). When nearing 238°F, there is fine overall bubbling with a coarser pattern as though the finer bubbled areas are being pulled down into the coarser ones. Remove from heat without jostling or stirring. Cool the candy to 110°F; you may hasten this process by placing the hot pan in a larger pan of cold water until the bottom of the pan has cooled. Add 2 tbsp butter and beat partially. Add 1 tsp vanilla, then beat until it begins to lose its sheen. The drip from the spoon holds its shape against the bottom of the pan when flipped over. Quickly add 1/2 - 1 cup broken nut meats, mix into a buttered pan, and cut into squares before it hardens. For chocolate fudge icing, use in all: 1 cup milk; beat until the icing reaches the right consistency to be spread.
